name: Certification Planning
summary: An upfront process for defining the certification basis, means of compliance, evidence packages, and authority engagement before technical work gets too deep.
whenToUseIt: Use it early in aircraft development, major modifications, and novel-technology programmes where late regulatory surprises are expensive.
steps: Define the product, operating context, and applicable regulations.
Agree the certification basis and special conditions with the authority.
Map each requirement to a means of compliance such as analysis, test, or inspection.
Assign ownership for evidence generation and review.
Track open issues, authority meetings, and compliance closure to approval.
example: A team adding a new electric-propulsion demonstrator to an existing aircraft cannot wait until flight test to think about compliance. Certification planning identifies which hazards, standards, and evidence streams must be addressed from day one.
